heading: Imposition of tax on heavy trucks and trailers sold at retail
label: 4051 Imposition of tax on heavy trucks and trailers sold at retail
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 4051, , , defined the price for which articles were sold for purposes of determining retailers excise taxes, prior to repeal by , , .act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 479Pub. L. 94–455, title XIX, § 1904(a)(1)(D)90 Stat. 1811Oct. 4, 1976

children.label: E
children.text: Tractors of the kind chiefly used for highway transportation in combination with a trailer or semitrailer.

children.heading: Exclusion for trucks weighing 33,000 pounds or less
children.label: 2 Exclusion for trucks weighing 33,000 pounds or less
children.text: The tax imposed by paragraph (1) shall not apply to automobile truck chassis and automobile truck bodies, suitable for use with a vehicle which has a gross vehicle weight of 33,000 pounds or less (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary).

children.heading: Exclusion for trailers weighing 26,000 pounds or less
children.label: 3 Exclusion for trailers weighing 26,000 pounds or less
children.text: The tax imposed by paragraph (1) shall not apply to truck trailer and semitrailer chassis and bodies, suitable for use with a trailer or semitrailer which has a gross vehicle weight of 26,000 pounds or less (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary).

children.heading: Exclusion for tractors weighing 19,500 pounds or less
children.label: 4 Exclusion for tractors weighing 19,500 pounds or less
A
children.text: such tractor has a gross vehicle weight of 19,500 pounds or less (as determined by the Secretary), and

children.label: B
children.text: such tractor, in combination with a trailer or semitrailer, has a gross combined weight of 33,000 pounds or less (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: Sale of trucks, etc., treated as sale of chassis and body
children.label: 5 Sale of trucks, etc., treated as sale of chassis and body
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a sale of an automobile truck or truck trailer or semitrailer shall be considered to be a sale of a chassis and of a body described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Separate purchase of truck or trailer and parts and accessories therefor
children.label: b Separate purchase of truck or trailer and parts and accessories therefor
A
children.text: the owner, lessee, or operator of any vehicle which contains an article taxable under subsection (a) installs (or causes to be installed) any part or accessory on such vehicle, and

children.label: B
children.text: such installation is not later than the date 6 months after the date such vehicle (as it contains such article) was first placed in service,

children.label: A
children.text: the part or accessory installed is a replacement part or accessory, or

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate price of the parts and accessories (and their installation) described in paragraph (1) with respect to any vehicle does not exceed $1,000 (or such other amount or amounts as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe).

children.heading: Installers secondarily liable for tax
children.label: 3 Installers secondarily liable for tax
children.text: The owners of the trade or business installing the parts or accessories shall be secondarily liable for the tax imposed by paragraph (1).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: c Termination
children.text: On and after , the taxes imposed by this section shall not apply.October 1, 2028

children.heading: Credit against tax for tire tax
children.label: d Credit against tax for tire tax

children.text: tires are sold on or in connection with the sale of any article, and
tax is imposed by this subchapter on the sale of such tires,
